---
title: "Compute Climate Normals"
engine: jupyter
jupyter: myproj
---

Aggregate temperature and precipitation data for each climate period by longitude, latitude and month number producing a single value per grid cell and month number.  Reshape data from long form to wide form (one row per grid cell with each monthly value in a separate column) to facillite the deriving of classification metrics.

### ***Climate Periods***

```{python}
#| label: climate_periods
# Climatological Reference Period
crp = "crp"
crp_beg = 1961
crp_end = 1990
# Recent Climate Period
rcp = "rcp"
rcp_beg = 2011
rcp_end = 2020
```

### ***Packages***

```{python}
#| label: packages
import xarray as xr
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import os
from pathlib import Path
from datetime import datetime
```

### ***Directories***

```{python}
#| label: directories
dir_rd = "_data/01_era5_download/"
dir_wr = "_data/02_climate_normals/"
```

### ***Read Land Sea Mask***

```{python}
#| label: read_land_sea_mask
fil = "land_sea_mask.nc"
pth = Path(dir_rd) / fil
ds = xr.open_dataset(
    pth,
    engine="h5netcdf",
    chunks="auto", 
    decode_cf=True
)
df_lsm = ds["lsm"].to_dataframe().reset_index()
df_lsm = df_lsm.rename(columns={"longitude": "lon", "latitude": "lat"})
df_lsm = df_lsm[["lon","lat","lsm"]]
```

### ***Functions***

```{python}
#| label: fn_compute_climate_normals
def fn_compute_climate_normals(
    cp: str,
    cv: str,
    vnm: str):
    fil = cp + "_" + cv + "_monthly.nc"
    pth = Path(dir_rd) / fil
    ds = xr.open_dataset(
        pth,
        engine="h5netcdf",
        chunks="auto", 
        decode_cf=True
    )
    ds = ds.drop_vars("number")
    # extract month number from time variable
    ds = ds.assign_coords(mth=ds["valid_time"].dt.month)
    # group by month number
    grp_vnm_da = ds[vnm].groupby("mth").mean("valid_time", keep_attrs=True)
    # convert to pandas df
    df_vnm = grp_vnm_da.to_dataframe().reset_index()
    # rename lon and lat columns
    df = df_vnm.rename(columns={"longitude": "lon", "latitude": "lat"})

    # convert lon values from 0 to 360  to -180 to 180
    df["lon"] = np.where(df["lon"] > 180, df["lon"] - 360, df["lon"])
    # modify variables based on type: t = temperature, p = precipitation
    if cv == "t":
        # convert temperature from kelvin to celsius
        df["t"] = df["t2m"] - 273.15
        # select columns to keep
        df = df[["lon", "lat", "mth", "t"]]
    elif cv == "p":
        # since era5 precipitation is stored as a daily average, multiply by days in month
        ds["ndy"] = ds["valid_time"].dt.days_in_month
        # climate perdiod average number of days in each month number (12 values)
        da_ndy = ds["ndy"].groupby("mth").mean("valid_time", keep_attrs=True)
        # convert to df before merging
        df_ndy = da_ndy.to_dataframe().reset_index()
        # merge with precipation df
        df = df.merge(df_ndy[["mth", "ndy"]], on="mth", how="left")
        # conversion from daily avg in m to monthly avg in mm
        df["p"] = df["tp"] * df["ndy"] * 1000
        # select columns to keep
        df = df[["lon", "lat", "mth", "p"]]
    # downcast columns to reduce size
    df = df.astype({"lon": "float32", "lat": "float32"})
    df = df.astype({"mth": "int8"})
    return(df)
```

```{python}
#| label: fn_pivot_climate_normals
def fn_pivot_climate_normals(
    df: pd.DataFrame,
    cp: str,
    cv: str):
    # Pivot to wide form
    wide_df = df.pivot(index=["lon", "lat"], columns="mth", values=cv)
    # Ensure columns are in month order 1–12
    wide_df = wide_df.reindex(columns=range(1, 13))
    # Rename columns 1..12 to m1..m12
    wide_df.columns = [f"m{m}" for m in wide_df.columns]
    # Reset index to keep lon and lat as normal columns
    wide_df = wide_df.reset_index()

    # add land sea mask based on lon and lat
    wide_df = wide_df.merge(df_lsm, on=["lon", "lat"],  how="left")
    # Save as parquet file
    fil = cp + "_" + cv + "_climate_normals.parquet"
    pth = Path(dir_wr) / fil
    wide_df.to_parquet(pth, index=False)
    return(wide_df)
```

### ***Compute Climate Normals***

```{python}
#| label: compute_climate_normals
start = datetime.now()
df1 = fn_compute_climate_normals(crp, "t", "t2m")
df2 = fn_compute_climate_normals(crp, "p", "tp")
df3 = fn_compute_climate_normals(rcp, "t", "t2m")
df4 = fn_compute_climate_normals(rcp, "p", "tp")
wide_df1 = fn_pivot_climate_normals(df1, crp, "t")
wide_df2 = fn_pivot_climate_normals(df2, crp, "p")
wide_df3 = fn_pivot_climate_normals(df3, rcp, "t")
wide_df4 = fn_pivot_climate_normals(df4, rcp, "p")
end = datetime.now()
print("elapsed time: " + str(end - start))
```
